$schema_comment: Sample config for an Anthropic-cloud /schedule runner (or any cron-driven runner) calling teammate's v0.8 sync routines. The runner reads this file, picks routines whose 'cron' fires now, runs `teammate sync <name>`, and stages the resulting markdown files as a PR via its own scoped tokens. The agent itself never holds tokens; this file is what the runner consumes to know which sources to pull and where to drop the staged drafts.

routines.name: confluence_sync
routines.description: Monday 04:00 UTC. Pulls the declared Confluence pages and stages a PR.

routines.name: jira_sync
routines.description: Monday 04:00 UTC. Pulls Jira issues matching the configured JQL and stages a PR.

routines.name: slack_sync
routines.description: Daily 04:00 UTC. Pulls pinned messages from the declared Slack channels.

routines.name: web_pull
routines.description: Sunday 05:00 UTC. Pulls a curated allowlist of public docs into the brain.

notes: The agent never holds tokens. The runner reads each artifact file the agent stages and opens the PR with its own scoped credentials.
Every sync routine writes PR drafts only — humans review and merge.
web_pull enforces a domain allowlist by default. Empty allowlist refuses everything (default-deny).
Slack pins are pre-resolved by the runner via the user's existing Slack MCP server. The agent only formats them.
